_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that water moves through the environment in a cycle — falling as rain, flowing through rivers, evaporating and forming clouds again.

_source.w: We are learning that water moves through the environment in a cycle — falling as rain, flowing through rivers, evaporating and forming clouds again.
y: The water cycle explains where our drinking water comes from and why we need to look after it.
Understanding this cycle helps us understand weather, floods, droughts and how ecosystems work.
Every drop of water on Earth has been cycling for billions of years — the water we drink today was once rain long ago.
eg: Building a mini water cycle in a sealed container with water, plastic wrap and a lamp as the sun.
Tracing the journey of a raindrop from cloud to river to ocean and back.
Watching condensation form on a cold glass of water on a warm day.
Drawing and labelling the water cycle: evaporation, condensation, precipitation, collection.
Discussing where our school's water supply comes from.
Observing a puddle over a few days and seeing it slowly evaporate.
